rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. MICHAEL E. JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 8, 2022 line 1. CROSS-DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for the cross-complainant to allege facts showing compliance with the Government Claims Act or facts showing excuse for non-compliance. Causes of action as currently alleged do not support exception under Krainock v. Superior Court (1990) 216 Cal.App.3d 1473. Cross-Complainant shall have 10 days to file an amended cross-complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PHUONG T. NGUYEN VS. LONDON BREED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Phuong T. Nguyen'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider Its Ruling On Order Filed From May 27, 2022 Concerning Plaintiff'S Demurrer To Answer And Motion To Strike Answer; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Supporting Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 8, 2022 line 6. PLAINTIFF PHUONG T. NGUYEN'S MOTION TO RECONSIDER ITS RULING ON ORDER FILED FROM MAY 27, 2022 CONCERNING PLAINTIFF'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER AND MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER is continued to September 12, 2022. The parties filed a stipulation to continue to September 9, 2022. No courtesy copy of the stipulation was provided to the Court and no proposed order was lodged. The Court notes that an unauthorized e-mail communication was received from the Defendant, it was deleted. In the future such defective submissions (lacking courtesy copies and proposed orders) will not be considered. The matter is continued to September 12, 2022 to accommodate Plaintiff's request for a Court reporter. No court reporter is available on September 9, 2022. No court reporter will be provided on August 8, 2022 in light of the stipulated continuance. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN GATEWAY CENTER, SPE, LLC, A LIMITED VS. ALYSSA LYNCH, AN INDIVIDUAL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 8, 2022 line 4. HEARING ON MARIE NICOLO'S CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION is DENIED. Proof of Service on file indicates that a Pre-Judgment Claim of Right to Possession was served on June 18, 2022 and this UD action is based on a notice to pay or quit.
